title: CPython imports can pass flake8 but fail on MicroPython
symptoms: Device raises ImportError for typing, pathlib, dataclasses, logging, or asyncio
PC tests pass but firmware fails at boot
check_mpy_imports.py --include-lib reports scaffold rotating_logger.py CPython logging fallback as a hard error
wrong_pattern: Importing CPython-only modules inside firmware runtime code, or treating CPython-only compatibility branches as runtime imports without checking their guard.
correct_pattern: Use MicroPython modules, project local helpers, or simple built-ins. Use uasyncio for firmware async code. Allow CPython-only imports only when they are unreachable on MicroPython, such as an else branch guarded by _IS_MICROPYTHON=False.
fix_guidance: Use uasyncio instead of asyncio in firmware runtime.
Treat try uasyncio / except ImportError import asyncio as MPY_IMPORT_CPYTHON_FALLBACK warning, not a hard failure.
Treat if _IS_MICROPYTHON: MicroPython branch else: import logging as MPY_IMPORT_CPYTHON_ONLY_GUARD warning when the CPython import is unreachable on MicroPython.
Keep direct runtime import logging, import asyncio, import pathlib, import dataclasses, import typing as MPY_IMPORT_UNSUPPORTED.
